description: LockBit is a cybercriminal group that operates a ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) model, first appearing on a Russian-language cybercrime forum in January 2020. The group encrypts victims' data and threatens to leak it publicly if ransoms are not paid. It was the most prolific ransomware in 2022, responsible for 44% of global ransomware incidents in early 2023. In February 2024, law enforcement seized its dark web sites, but the group continued attacks and attempted a comeback.

country_links.evidence_quote: United States, India and Brazil are the top targeted countries.
country_links.context: Brazil is one of the top targeted countries for LockBit attack attempts.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 21 May 2024, LockBit claimed responsibility for an attack on the corporate offices of Canadian retail chain London Drugs...
country_links.context: LockBit claimed responsibility for an attack on the corporate offices of Canadian retail chain London Drugs.

country_links.evidence_quote: In June 2024, the LockBit gang attacked the University Hospital Center in Zagreb , the largest medical facility in Croatia . ... LockBit claimed to have exfiltrated a large number of files...
country_links.context: LockBit attacked the University Hospital Center in Zagreb, Croatia, exfiltrating files and demanding ransom.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 2022, the group's hackers claimed cyberattacks against 28 organizations, 12 of which involved French organizations. Among them, the Corbeil Essonnes hospital was targeted...
country_links.context: LockBit claimed cyberattacks against 28 organizations, 12 of which involved French organizations, including the Corbeil Essonnes hospital.

country_links.evidence_quote: On May 16, 2023, the hacker group claimed responsibility for attacking the Hong Kong branch of the Chinese newspaper China Daily .
country_links.context: LockBit claimed responsibility for attacking the Hong Kong branch of the Chinese newspaper China Daily.

country_links.evidence_quote: United States, India and Brazil are the top targeted countries.
country_links.context: India is one of the top targeted countries for LockBit attack attempts.

country_links.evidence_quote: On November 10, 2022, the LockBit 3.0 group published on the darknet a 9.5 GB archive with stolen information on Thales contracts in Italy and Malaysia.
country_links.context: LockBit published stolen information on Thales contracts in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: In July 2023, LockBit attacked the Port of Nagoya in Japan, which handles 10% of the country's trade. The attack forced a shutdown of container operations.
country_links.context: LockBit attacked the Port of Nagoya in Japan, forcing a shutdown of container operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: On November 10, 2022, the LockBit 3.0 group published on the darknet a 9.5 GB archive with stolen information on Thales contracts in Italy and Malaysia.
country_links.context: LockBit published stolen information on Thales contracts in Malaysia.

country_links.evidence_quote: On its site on the dark web, LockBit stated that it was 'located in the Netherlands, completely apolitical and only interested in money'.
country_links.context: LockBit stated on its dark web site that it is located in the Netherlands.

country_links.evidence_quote: One person was arrested in Ukraine, one in Poland, and two in the United States.
country_links.context: One person was arrested in Poland in connection with LockBit.

country_links.evidence_quote: One person was arrested in Ukraine, one in Poland, and two in the United States.
country_links.context: One person was arrested in Ukraine in connection with LockBit.

country_links.evidence_quote: In October 2022, the LockBit group claimed responsibility for an attack on Pendragon PLC , a group of automotive retailers in the UK...
country_links.context: LockBit claimed responsibility for an attack on Pendragon PLC, a group of automotive retailers in the UK.

country_links.evidence_quote: In the United States between January 2020 and May 2023, LockBit was used in approximately 1,700 ransomware attacks...
country_links.context: LockBit was used in approximately 1,700 ransomware attacks in the United States between January 2020 and May 2023.
